content:
French translation of my original message because I see that you are from Paris:
Un puzzle inversé amusant ! Cela dit, je rejoins @5DN1L pour dire qu'un peu plus de peaufinage serait nécessaire.
1) J'aimerais que le cas où a = 0 apparaisse plus tôt. Ce n'est pas très agréable de devoir attendre l'exécution de 6 cas de test avant de repérer cette erreur. Je placerais ce cas en 3e ou 4e position, plutôt qu'en 7e.
2) Comme l'a souligné @5DN1L, il faudrait éviter d'utiliser des émojis dans les cas de test et les validateurs, car cela peut poser problème avec certains langages de programmation.
3) Par ailleurs, toutes les remarques formulées par @5DN1L mériteraient également d'être prises en compte.
Merci!

Goal
* Please add "(REVERSE MODE)" at the end.

Stub generator
* Please fix the typo: whish => wish.

Cases
* Some cases have no names or they are wrongly numbered. Please fix those case names.

* Please avoid using non-ASCII characters in inputs and outputs because they require special handling in some programming languages, and that might be beyond beginners' knowledge.

* Please change the single-space case to use a sentence as input instead. If you want to keep the original case, please create a new pair of cases for it.

Thanks.

Thanks a lot for your comment.

As @vince_mx said, I am french then sorry for the 'whish'.

I didn't know about non-ASCII characters echues, so thank you to have noticed and reported it.
Good evening (it is evening in UK, isn't it ?)

Thank you for your comment and for the french translation (it is very kind from you)

I agree with you about the a = 0 case.

Just like you (if you still are), I am in high school.

Goodbye.

Thank you for making the changes. Yes, it was evening.

Cases
* Please revise Test 4 and Validator 4, as their inputs also contain non-ASCII characters (e.g. §, ê, ç and à). Please use only characters with ASCII values in the range of 32 to 126.

Thanks again.
